column1: # Tab-separated: name  type  repo  tag  mode  dest  flags  sha256
#   mode    – "all-assets" to grab every file, or exact asset filename
#   flags   – dash for none; comma-separated: executable, allow-text
#   sha256  – lowercase hex SHA256 of the downloaded artifact, or
#   Real SHA256 values are required for release-grade sync.  TODO_SHA256 is
#   accepted in default/dev mode (sync-binaries.sh prints a [WARN]) but FAILS
#   under STRICT_CHECKSUMS=1 (or --strict-checksums).  Use strict mode for
#   Per-asset checksums are NOT supported for mode=all-assets.  For all-assets
#   rows, sha256 must be TODO_SHA256; supplying a real hash for an all-assets
#   row will FAIL with a clear error.  Pin individual artifacts by switching
#   When the chosen `mode` filename ends in `.gz`, sync-binaries.sh decompresses
#   the verified archive into a sibling executable (same dir, suffix dropped)
#   and chmod +x's it.  The original `.gz` is preserved so the verified release
#   artifact stays on disk.  The sha256 always pertains to the compressed `.gz`
#   bytes that were downloaded, never to the decompressed payload.
#   1. Pick the desired upstream tag at https://github.com/jpillora/chisel
#   3. Copy the sha256 next to chisel_<TAG>_linux_amd64.gz into the row below.
#   4. Update tag and mode to match the new asset filename.
# After editing: labctl sync   (or scripts/sync-binaries.sh directly)
# all-assets is permitted only as a non-strict / dev-only pattern (no per-asset
# checksums yet — strict release sanity will FAIL on any all-assets row):
